name: Religious Coalition For Community Renewal
description_short: Faith-based nonprofit housing counselor and affordable housing developer in Charleston, WV serving low-income families with free counseling, home repair assistance, and rental support.
description_long:
The Religious Coalition for Community Renewal (RCCR) was established in 1987 as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization in Charleston, West Virginia. It operates as a membership organization comprising over 30 interfaith religious congregations working cooperatively to address affordable housing obstacles in the community. The organization is headquartered at 1117 Quarrier Street in Charleston and maintains a clear mission focused on community renewal through housing solutions.

RCCR offers multiple housing-related services including HUD-approved housing counseling (267 families served in 2025), free home repair assistance through its Healthy Homes Program for residents in the 25302 zip code, rental assistance referrals via 211, and permanent supportive housing for formerly unhoused individuals. The organization also facilitates new home mortgages (generating $2 million in 2025) for first-time homebuyers, has created 66 affordable rental units, constructed 3 single-family homes, and maintains 12 properties in predevelopment. Additionally, RCCR operates recovery housing serving 26 residents with 6 graduates in 2025, and provides extensive community support including clothing distribution and personal belongings storage for unsheltered neighbors.

What distinguishes RCCR is its integrated approach combining housing counseling with direct development of affordable units and supportive services. Unlike mortgage lenders that focus solely on financing, RCCR addresses barriers holistically through free counseling, home repair aid, and wraparound services like recovery housing and utility assistance. The organization demonstrates significant community impact, serving 750+ individuals through outreach in 2025 and leveraging 2,500+ volunteer hours.

RCCR is best suited for low-to-moderate income West Virginia residents seeking free housing counseling, home repair assistance, or affordable rental/purchase options in the Charleston area. The primary limitation is geographic scope—services are concentrated in Charleston and surrounding areas, with the Healthy Homes Program specifically limited to zip code 25302. While they facilitate mortgages, they function primarily as a nonprofit counselor and developer rather than a direct mortgage lender.
pros: Free HUD-approved housing counseling for 267 families (2025)
Free home repair assistance through Healthy Homes Program for qualifying 25302 residents
Directly facilitates $2 million in new home mortgages annually with focus on first-time buyers
Operates 66+ affordable rental units created/maintained by the organization
Comprehensive support including recovery housing, utility/rental assistance referrals, and emergency belongings storage
Interfaith nonprofit with 30+ congregation members providing volunteer oversight and community connection
Transparent 2025 impact reporting showing 750+ individuals served across multiple programs
cons: Services geographically limited to Charleston, WV area; Healthy Homes Program restricted to zip code 25302
Functions as counselor and developer rather than direct mortgage lender—mortgage facilitation role is unclear regarding terms, rates, or approval process
No online application or intake system visible; requires phone calls (304-346-6398) or in-person contact for services
Recovery housing capacity very limited (26 residents served in 2025) relative to broader homelessness in region
Website lacks details on eligibility criteria, income limits, or timeline for services like home repair assistance
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Charleston, WV seeking free pre-purchase counseling and mortgage facilitation
Low-income 25302 residents needing free home repairs and weatherization assistance
Individuals exiting homelessness or recovery situations seeking stable permanent housing with supportive services
Renters in the Charleston area facing utility or rental payment crises (via 211 referral network)
diagnosis: RCCR is best for low-income West Virginia residents seeking free, comprehensive housing counseling combined with direct access to affordable housing solutions in the Charleston area. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—services are concentrated in Charleston with some programs restricted to specific zip codes, and the organization functions primarily as a nonprofit developer and counselor rather than a direct mortgage lender.
services: Free home repair assistance through Healthy Homes Program (25302 zip code only)
Utility and rental assistance referrals via 211 hotline
First-time homebuyer mortgage facilitation and support
Affordable rental unit development and management (66+ units)
Recovery housing with supportive services (26 residents)
Emergency belongings storage for unsheltered individuals (917,178 lbs. in 2025)
Clothing and personal item distribution to homeless populations
Community housing fairs and outreach (350+ served in 2025)
Property development and predevelopment support (12 properties)
Volunteer coordination and community mobilization (2,500+ hours in 2025)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
